data.title: M-tron signals expanded acquisition strategy and $76.4M backlog amid strong defense demand
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: M-tron Industries, Inc. (MPTI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Cameron Pforr, CFO & CEO, highlighted a strong finish to fiscal 2025, emphasizing "continued strength in M-tron sales and good financial performance for Q4 FY 2025." He noted, "Bookings growth was particularly strong with a 62% increase in backlog over the 2024 year-end figure."
  • Pforr stated, "Our revenues continue to be driven by defense-related orders and strong improvement in the commercial aircraft or what we call the avionics sector."
  • The company made "strategic investments in research and development and continued our efforts to increase the market profile of the company."
  • Pforr referenced the recent signing of 7-year production agreements between Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, and the Department of War, with M-tron as a significant vendor to both companies' precision-guided munition programs, calling this "one of the most significant growth areas for M-tron."
  • On capital structure, Pforr stated, "We added $10.7 million of cash through operations. In December 2025, we sent a new loan agreement with Fifth Third Bank...Currently, we have no debt outstanding and haven't drawn upon this line."
  • The company closed a warrant offering, raising $27.5 million in net proceeds for potential acquisitions. Pforr said, "This offering is designed to support accretive acquisitions, the ability to perform carve-outs or participate in carve-outs, transactions of scale, the pursuit of strategic investments and also to expand our internal capabilities to meet demand."
  • Pforr announced an upcoming rights offering to further expand acquisition flexibility, with details on share pricing to be disclosed next week.
  • Pforr noted, "At the end of the period, M-tron had $20.9 million of cash on the balance sheet, with an additional $27.5 million being transferred early January 2026 to the company from our transfer agent as warrant proceeds."

Outlook

  • Management expects continued expansion of the defense program business, particularly in radar and electronic warfare, stating, "Two of the areas where we expect strong growth in '26 and '27 are radar and electronic warfare."
  • Pforr identified "strong growth throughout the year from commercial avionic shipments as we serve key suppliers to both Boeing and Airbus."
  • The rights offering is expected to launch in the coming week, with the subscription price anticipated at a 10% to 12% discount to the trailing 5-day volume weighted average price per share.

Financial Results

  • Total revenues for Q4 2025 were $14.2 million, an 11.2% increase over the same period last year, driven by "strong defense program product as well as avionics shipments."
  • Gross margins for Q4 2025 were 46.9% compared to 47.2% in Q4 2024, impacted by tariffs and product mix.
  • Net income per diluted share was $0.99 for Q4 2025, compared to $0.73 in the prior year's period.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $4.5 million for Q4 2025, a 46.8% increase over the previous year's quarter.
  • Backlog at quarter-end was $76.4 million, up from $47.2 million at the end of 2024.
  • For fiscal 2025, total revenues were $54.4 million, with gross margins at 44.4% and net income at $8.4 million, or $2.62 per diluted share.
  • The company reversed $860,000 in previously accrued incentive compensation expense, positively impacting Q4 results by 6%.

Q&A

  • Anja Soderstrom, Sidoti & Company, LLC: "What's the motivation for this rights offering?" Cameron Pforr: "It's really more being driven by some of the pressure from the industry, from the Department of War on primes to become more nimble...having a stronger balance sheet is going to allow M-tron to take advantage of the opportunities."
  • Anja Soderstrom: "How should we think about gross margin for next year?" Pforr: "We do expect a slightly more favorable tariff environment next year...In 2025, tariffs impacted our gross margins by about 1%. We do expect them to be a little bit less in '26 and forward."
  • Anja Soderstrom: "What was most of the growth [in backlog] coming from?" Pforr: "The backlog was driven by defense and aerospace and then avionics. Both of those were strong developer growth areas in the backlog."
  • Unknown Analyst: "How meaningful is the drone exposure today in your business? And can it become a more material part of the mix over time?" Pforr: "We do sell solutions and components to several of the manufacturers of drones...In the prior year, we typically do like $1.5 million to $2 million of revenue in that space. But I think that's going to grow."
  • Unknown Analyst: "Can you quantify the radar systems revenue for the 2026?" Pforr: "I think it will probably total around roughly $4 million probably."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the company’s acquisition strategy, gross margin outlook, and backlog growth, maintaining a neutral tone with probing questions about forward plans and market exposure.
  • Management maintained a confident and slightly optimistic tone in prepared remarks, with Pforr stating, "We are well positioned to continue to perform well with a significant upside potential given Department of War's actions and the recent conflicts in Iran." During Q&A, Pforr was measured but direct, acknowledging uncertainty in tariffs and product mix.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remains probing but less focused on operational risks, while management appears more confident due to the strengthened balance sheet and expanded backlog.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize larger acquisition opportunities and flexibility through additional capital-raising, compared to the prior quarter's focus on R&D and operational execution.
  • Strategic focus has expanded, with management now highlighting inorganic growth and strategic partnerships alongside organic growth.
  • Key metrics improved: Q4 revenue and EBITDA both increased, and backlog grew substantially from $58.8 million at Q3 end to $76.4 million at Q4 end.
  • Management tone has shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 to a more assertive and confident stance in Q4, supported by financial results and market opportunities.
  • Analysts' questions evolved from operational details and partnerships in Q3 to capital allocation and long-term demand visibility in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified tariffs and product mix as ongoing challenges impacting gross margins, with Pforr noting, "We do expect a slightly more favorable tariff environment next year...it's a moving target."
  • The company acknowledged the impact of new product introductions on margins and referenced uncertainty in timing for increased production related to new defense contracts.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of margin improvement and the materiality of revenue from drone and radar systems.

Final Takeaway

M-tron's Q4 2025 call outlined a company leveraging strong defense sector demand to expand its backlog 62% year-over-year, while proactively raising capital for acquisitions through a rights offering and warrant proceeds. Management emphasized both organic and inorganic growth strategies, highlighted by upcoming opportunities in radar, electronic warfare, and commercial avionics, and pointed to a strengthened balance sheet as a foundation for future expansion. The company faces ongoing challenges from tariffs and evolving product mix, but remains confident in its ability to capitalize on new defense contracts and strategic market trends.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Brown-Forman jumps on report Pernod Ricard weighing purchase
data.description:
Brown-Forman (BF.B) soared 11% on a report that Pernod Ricard SA (PDRDF) is weighing a potential purchase of the maker of Jack Daniel's whiskey.

Pernod Ricard (PDRDF) has had some initial talks with Brown-Forman (BF.B) about a deal, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

Deliberations are ongoing, and there’s no certainty they will result in a deal, the people told Bloomberg. 

Pernod Ricard and Brown-Forman didn’t immediately respond to Bloomberg requests for comment.

data.title: Hertz and Avis rally as airport gridlock fuels demand for car rentals
data.description:
Airport security delays during the partial government shutdown and subsequent shortage of Transportation Security Administration agents are giving a nice boost to the car rental business as travelers ditch air travel for road trips.  

According to data compiled by Hertz (HTZ) reported by TravelPulse, search traffic to the car rental’s website has increased ~15%, with results indicating 33% of rentals are for road trips and driving vacations. The trend mirrors a comparable spike in car rentals during the last government shutdown in November.

To help travelers avoid the airport snarls, Hertz (HTZ) is recommending travelers book at neighborhoods, prepay for fuel to lock in prices, and add flight information if necessary to ensure availability in the event of long airport delays and missed flights. 

Hertz (HTZ) is also offering 25% off last-minute reservations and one-way rentals at airports.

The spike in demand for rental cars is driving up shares of Hertz (HTZ) and Avis Budget Group (CAR), both adding double-digit gains on Thursday to a four-day advance of 26% and 40%, respectively.

data.title: Semiconductor equipment stocks may 'peak' in late 2026 based on past cycles: BNP
data.description:
Semiconductor equipment stocks may start to “peak” in the latter part of 2026, if history is any guidance, investment firm BNP Paribas said on Thursday. 

“Semicap stocks have rallied 2x in six months, with the share price rally closely correlated with the increase in memory prices,” analyst David O’Connor wrote in a note to clients. “64% of the share price increase has stemmed from multiple expansion (now at record levels) and only 27% from cons. EPS revisions. While there is much debate on the sustainability of this rally, we remain in the camp that believes memory continues to be a commodity and a cost-plus business. We analyze our Semicap names through the lens of previous cycles and flag the risk to our stocks in a correction scenario (c. 50%). With stocks trading at record multiples and discounting mid- to high-teens WFE growth in 2026-27, it's hard to see what drives stocks higher.”

O'Connor has a Neutral rating on Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX), and KLA Corp. (KLAC).

Delving deeper, O'Connor said the previous cycles have seen share price peaks come three quarters after the multiples peaked. And with that, revenues peaked one quarter after that, and then memory prices corrected around the same time “meaningful” additional supply became available.

“In the last two corrections (Cloud cycle 2017, COVID 2021), memory prices fell an average of 55% and Semicap share prices fell 43%, primarily driven by multiple compression. Implication for current cycle: implies share price peak in late 2026 or early 2027 With meaningful memory supply coming online in H2'27, overlaying the above Semicap cycle behavior on the current cycle suggests a memory price correction in H2'27. This would be preceded by a Semicap rev. peak in mid-2027, with share price peaking in late 2026/early 2027 and PE multiple peaking in Q2'26. We note the PE multiple appears to have already peaked for Semicap [companies].”

data.title: PayPal, Stripe, Visa, Mastercard are the latest to be warned about debanking by Trump administration
data.description:
PayPal (PYPL) (PYPL:CA), Stripe (STRIP), Visa (V) (VISA:CA), and Mastercard (MA) (MA:CA) are the latest financial firms to be warned about debanking by the Trump administration regulators.

The Federal Trade Commission has issued debanking warning letters to the CEOs of the four payment processors.

U.S. President Donald Trump was said to have filed a $5B lawsuit against JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and its longtime CEO Jamie Dimon, alleging the bank cut ties with him and his businesses for political reasons.

Last year, Trump's privately-owned conglomerate filed a lawsuit against Capital One (COF), accusing the bank of wrongfully terminating more than 300 company accounts in 2021.

"Full participation in commerce and public life necessarily requires that law-abiding individuals can access, and freely participate in, our financial system," said FTC Chairman Andrew Ferguson in his latest letter.

"That is why President Trump's August 7, 2025, Executive Order on debanking makes clear that it is unacceptable to debank law-abiding citizens due to political affiliations, religious beliefs, or lawful business activities," said Ferguson.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Dundee Preference shares Series 3 GAAP EPS of $6.73
data.description:

  • During the fourth quarter of 2025, the Corporation recognized royalty revenue of $1.3 million from the Borborema Gold Project. Subsequent to year-end 2025, the Corporation sold the net smelter royalty for total consideration of $65.8 million and will recognize a $47.5 million gain in the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Trump says Iran’s gift was the passage of 10 oil boats through Hormuz
data.description:
President Donald Trump said Iran has allowed the passage of 10 oil boats through the Strait of Hormuz this week as a gift to the U.S.

During a cabinet meeting, the president said Iran presented this “gift” to show the U.S. that “we’re real and solid and we’re there.”

This comes as Iran had reportedly said the Islamic country would not accept a ceasefire and had denied direct talks with the U.S.

data.title: Carnival faces fuel cost headwinds ahead of Q1 results
data.description:
When Carnival (CCL) is set to announce first-quarter results on Friday, Wall Street expects the company to post EPS of $0.18, implying a 48.6% decline, while revenue is expected to slip 3% to $6.14B for the quarter.

The cruise operator expects 2026 net income of over $3.45 billion, a 12% or $0.23 per share year-over-year increase.

Investors will be looking for the impact of the war between the U.S., Israel, and Iran on the company’s revenue yield due to higher fuel and energy costs.

CCL shares have slipped 15.7% since the start of the year, compared to a 3.7% decline in the broader S&P 500 index (SP500), due to higher fuel costs amid rising Middle East tensions.

However, Morgan Stanley analyst Jamie Rollo noted that CCL’s decline is in line with declines during the Iraq War, the Russia-Ukraine war, and the Arab Spring, and all those down periods were followed by material rebounds.

The brokerage firm upgraded Carnival to Overweight due to an attractive risk-reward balance, saying that CCL, with internal levers and strong FCF, and the industry, with a lower order book, are in better shape than in prior downturns.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating and analysts, along with Wall Street analysts, are bullish, rating CCL a Buy.

Susquehanna maintained its Positive rating for CCL while decreasing its price target from $40 to $30.

The brokerage firm stated CCL is unique among its cruise peers in that it does not hedge fuel. However, it believes that elevated fuel prices are likely to result in upside pressure on cruise fares via higher ticket prices, selective onboard price increases, and, in certain instances, changes in itineraries.

Additionally, the value gap between cruises and land-based vacations remains intact, which should help support demand amid an uncertain macro and geopolitical backdrop, the brokerage firm added.

Over the last two years, CCL has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 88% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen three upward revisions versus four downward moves, while revenue estimates have seen six upward revisions against three downward revisions.

data.title: Wall Street trades lower as attention remains focused on the Middle East conflict
data.description:
Wall Street edged lower on Thursday as investors kept a close eye on escalating U.S.-Iran tensions after Tehran dismissed a U.S. proposal aimed at ending the conflict.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was last at -0.4%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.8%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.1%. 

From a sector-by-sector standpoint, seven of the 11 S&P segments were in the red, with communication services at the bottom. At the other end, energy has been the strongest performer of the session. 

Treasury yields rose across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 5 basis points to 4.38%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) added 5 basis points to 3.94%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) edged up 3 basis points to 4.93%. 

A senior political-security official has reportedly told Iranian news network Press TV that Iran has formally rejected the American proposal, insisting that Tehran will dictate the terms and timing of the war’s conclusion. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims data for the week ended March 21 rose by 5K to 210K, against the consensus of 210K. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Best Buy (BBY) climbed +3.9%, and shares of Applovin Corporation (APP) fell -8.1%.
